---
name: claude-agent-router
description: "Use when the user wants Claude-like subagents, role-based delegation, or multi-agent orchestration. Route the task to one or more repo skills generated from .claude/agents/*/*.md."
---

<!-- generated by scripts/sync_claude_to_codex.py -->

# Claude Agent Router

1. Map the request to the smallest set of matching agent bridge skills.
2. Read only the matched `.claude/agents/*/*.md` files first.
3. If work can be split into independent threads, parallelize tool calls and keep each thread scoped to one role.
4. If the task is sequential, hand off between roles explicitly instead of mixing responsibilities.
5. Use these role groups:
- `coordination`: `chief-of-staff`
- `debugging`: `build-error-resolver`, `debugger`, `performance`
- `documentation`: `documenter`
- `exploration`: `code-explorer`
- `planning`: `architect`, `planner`
- `refactoring`: `refactorer`
- `review`: `code-reviewer`, `code-simplifier`, `database-reviewer`, `python-reviewer`, `security-reviewer`, `silent-failure-hunter`, `type-design-analyzer`
- `testing`: `e2e-runner`, `pr-test-analyzer`, `tdd-guide`, `tester`
- `tooling`: `agent-creator`, `plugin-validator`, `skill-reviewer`
6. Native Claude subagents are approximated here through role skills. Be explicit when a Claude-only workflow cannot be reproduced exactly.
